1.1: There was a man in the land of Uz; his name was Job. The man was perfect and righteous and worshiped God and departed from evil.
1.2: And seven sons and three daughters were born unto him
1.3: And his possessions were seven thousand sheep and three thousand camels, five hundred yokes of oxen, five hundred she donkeys and a very great and valuable estate, and the man was the greatest of all the sons of the East.
1.4: And his sons were going and making a feast in the house, each man on his day, and were sending and inviting their three sisters to eat and drink with them.
1.5: And it was that when the days of feasting came near, Job sent and hallowed them and arose early at dawn and offered up sacrifices according to the numbers of all of them, for Job said: “Perhaps my children have sinned and have cursed God in their heart.” Thus Job did all their days.
1.6: And the day came and the Sons of God came to stand before LORD JEHOVAH, and Satan came also in their midst to stand before LORD JEHOVAH.
1.7: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Satan: “From where have you come?” Satan answered and said to LORD JEHOVAH: “I have been going around the earth and I have been walking in it.”
1.8: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Satan: “Behold, you have set your heart on my Servant Job, for there is none like him in the earth, a man perfect and upright, and he worships God and departs from evil.”
1.9: And Satan answered and said to LORD JEHOVAH: “Does Job worship God for nothing?
1.10: You rested your hand upon him and upon his house and upon his sons and upon everything that he has in every place, and you have blessed the work of his hands, and his property has increased in the land.
1.11: But now reach your hand and touch everything that he has, he shall only curse you to your face!”
1.12: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Satan: “Behold, everything that he has is handed over into your hands, only do not stretch your hand upon him.” And Satan went out from before the face of LORD JEHOVAH
1.13: And the day came and the sons and daughters of Job were eating and drinking in the house of their elder brother
1.14: And a Messenger came to Job and said to him: “The oxen were driving plows and female donkeys were grazing by their side
1.15: And a band of robbers fell and took them, and the young men were killed by the sword, and I escaped, I alone, that I would tell you!”
1.16: And while he was speaking, another came and said to him: “The fire of God has fallen from Heaven and set fire to the sheep and the shepherds, and it burned them up, and I alone escaped that I would tell you!”
1.17: And while he was speaking, another came and said to him: “Chaldeans were divided up to three parts and they fell on the camels and they drove them and the young men they murdered by the sword, and I escaped alone that I may tell you!”
1.18: And while he was speaking, another came and said to him: “Your sons and your daughters were eating and drinking in their eldest brother’s house
1.19: And behold, a mighty wind came from the other side of the wilderness and struck the four corners of the house, and it fell on the boys, and they died, and I escaped, I alone, to tell you!”
1.20: And Job stood and ripped his cloak and shaved his head, and he fell on the ground and worshiped
1.21: And he said: “Naked I came out from the belly of my mother, and naked I shall return. LORD JEHOVAH has given and LORD JEHOVAH has taken away. Blessed shall be the name of LORD JEHOVAH!”
1.22: And in all these things Job did not sin, neither did he blaspheme against God.

2.1: And it was day, and the Sons of God came to stand before LORD JEHOVAH, and Satan came also in their midst to stand before LORD JEHOVAH
2.2: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Satan: “From where have you come?” Satan answered and said to LORD JEHOVAH: “I have encircled the Earth and I have walked in it!”
2.3: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Satan: “Behold, you have set your heart on my Servant Job, for there is none like him in the Earth, a man perfect and upright, and he worships God and departs from evil, and until now holds firmly to his perfection, and you provoke me against him that I would destroy him for nothing!”
2.4: And Satan answered and said to LORD JEHOVAH: “Skin in exchange for skin and everything that a man has he gives in exchange for his life, and he escapes
2.5: But reach your hand and touch his flesh or his bone, and he will curse you to your face!”
2.6: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Satan: “Now he is handed over into your hands, only save his life!”
2.7: And Satan went out from before the face of LORD JEHOVAH and struck Job with evil abscesses from the sole of his foot and unto his brain
2.8: And he took for himself a clay vessel to scrape himself and he was sitting down on the ashes
2.9: And his wife said to him: “Even now you hold to your integrity? Curse God and die!
2.10: He said to her: “You talk like one of the crazy women talks! Do we receive the good things of God, and will we not receive his bad things?” And in all these things Job did not sin, neither did he blaspheme God with his lips
2.11: And the three friends of Job heard of all this evil that had come upon him, and they made an appointment and they came to him, each man from his place: Eliphaz the Taymanite and Beldad the Shukhite and Tsuphar the Namthite, and they made an appointment as one to come and to speak with his heart and for his comfort
2.12: And they lifted up their eyes from a distance and they did not recognize him, and they lifted up their voice and wept, and they ripped, each man his cloak, and they sprinkled dust on their heads toward Heaven
2.13: And they dwelt with him on the ground seven days and seven nights, and there was none that said a word, because they saw that his plague was very severe with him

3.1: And after this, Job opened his mouth and cursed the day on which he was born
3.3: “Let the day in which I was born be destroyed, and the night in which it was said, ‘a man is conceived’
3.4: Let that day be dark and let it not be sought by God from above, neither let light shine upon it
3.5: Let darkness and the shadow of death cover it. Let a cloud sink upon it and let the bitterness of the day terrify it
3.6: Let that night cover it in blackness. That day not shall be counted in the number of the days of the year and in the number of the months it shall not enter
3.7: That night shall be destitute and a song shall not enter it
3.8: The cursers of the day shall curse it, those who are going to rouse up Leviathan
3.9: Let the stars of its dawning be darkened; let them look for light and it shall not be, and let it not see the rays of its dawning
3.10: Because it shut not the doors of the womb of my mother nor hid pain from my eyes
3.11: Why did I not die from the womb, and from conception why did I come out the threshold?
3.12: Why did the knees rear me and why did I suck from the breasts?
3.13: But doubtless now I had been sleeping and quiet, and I had been lying down and resting
3.14: With Kings and Rulers of The Earth who were building up desolations for themselves
3.15: Or with Rulers who had gold, and money fills their households
3.16: Or like a miscarriage buried where I had not been, and like embryos who did not see the light
3.17: There the evil have ceased from being restless, and there those who are weary in their lives are given rest
3.18: Together prisoners are calm and they do not hear the voice of the oppressor
3.19: The small and the great are there, and the Servant who is freed from his Master
3.20: Why is light given to sufferers and life to the bitter of soul
3.21: Who expect death, and it is not, and seek it like a treasure
3.22: Who rejoice and they assemble and they exult when they find the tomb
3.23: To a man whose way is hidden and God descends upon him?
3.24: Because before my bread my sighing entered, and my moanings are poured out like waters
3.25: Because the fear which I feared has come upon me, and that thing by which I was shaken has met me
3.26: I was not silent and I was not resting and I was not fallen down, and wrath came”

4.1: And Eliphaz the Taymanite answered and he said:
4.2: “If I begin to speak with you, will you be worn out? And who can withhold words from you?
4.3: Behold, you have instructed many, and you would strengthen the hands of the weak
4.4: Should not your words lift up the afflicted and you should strengthen the shaky knees?
4.5: Because now it has come upon you and you are troubled, and it has arrived upon you and you are terrified
4.6: Behold, this your religion is blaming you, also your hope and the integrity of your way
4.7: Remember now, who is innocent that is destroyed, and where are the upright who were put to shame?
4.8: As when I have seen, they who cultivate with sin and sow pain will reap it
4.9: And from the breath of God they shall be destroyed and from The Spirit of his wrath they shall be consumed
4.10: The roaring of a lion and the voice of the whelp of a lion and the teeth of lions were broken
4.11: A lion perishes from want, he has no prey, and the offspring of the lionesses are scattered
4.12: And to me an answer was given, and my ear received as a little from him
4.13: In quietness in a vision of the night in the falling of deep sleep on a man
4.14: An awesomeness chilled me, and a shaking, and the multitude of my bones were shaken
4.15: And a spirit passed before my face, and the hair of my flesh stood up
4.16: And I stood and I did not see, and there was no apparition before my eyes, and I heard a whisper and a voice that said:
4.17: Will man be declared more just than God, or is a man purer than his Maker?
4.18: Behold, he does not trust in his Servants and in his Angels he puts amazement
4.19: Also those dwelling in houses of clay that dwell in the dust will be laid low before the thick darkness
4.20: From dawn to evening they will be laid low, that they will not be established inhabitants to eternity, and they will be destroyed
4.21: Behold, he takes up their property from them, and their remnants among them will die, and not in wisdom.

5.1: Call now if there is one who answers you, and to whom of the holy ones will you turn?
5.2: Because passion kills a stupid man and anger kills a fool
5.3: I have seen the wicked who prospers and his dwelling is suddenly destroyed
5.4: His sons will be far from redemption and they will be humbled at the gate, and there is none who redeems them
5.5: What he reaps a famine will consume, and they will be poured out for thirst, and the thirsty break their possessions into pieces
5.6: Because falsehood does not come out from the dust, neither does evil spring forth from the ground
5.7: Because a son of man is born to trouble, and the offspring of birds to lift up wings
5.8: However, I shall seek for God, and to God I shall put my word
5.9: Who does great things without limit and wonders without number
5.10: Who gives rain on the face of the Earth and sends waters on the face of the streets
5.11: To put the humble in high place and the meek will be strengthened with redemption
5.12: He brings the ideas of the wise to nothing that their hands will not perform a scheme
5.13: He seizes wise men in their philosophy and the thinking of the crafty he brings to nothing
5.14: By day they will grope as when in darkness, and they will grope at noonday as at night
5.15: He saves the poor from the sword of their mouth and from the hand of the mighty
5.16: And the poor will have hope and the evil one will shut his mouth
5.17: His blessing to a man whom God reproves and the discipline of The Almighty let him not reject
5.18: Because he breaks and binds up and he strikes and his hands heal
5.19: In six troubles he will save you, and in the seventh evil will not approach to you
5.20: In famine he will save you from death, and in war from the hands of the sword
5.21: From the rod of the tongue you shall be sheltered, and you will not be afraid of ruin when it comes
5.22: Against looting and against famine you will laugh, and of animals of the field you will not be afraid
5.23: Because your covenant is with the stones of the wilderness, and the animal of the field is handed over to you
5.24: And you will know that your tent is secure and you will return to your dwelling and you will not sin
5.25: And you will know that your seed is abundant and your offspring as grass of the Earth
5.26: And you will enter the tomb gently, as a shock of corn comes up in its time
5.27: This we searched and thus it is that we heard it, and know for yourself

6.2: Oh, but that my anger were weighed and a piece of my mind in a scale together
6.3: Because now it is more than the sand of the seas, because of this, my words were restrained
6.4: Because the bolts of The Almighty are in my flesh and their heat drinks up my spirit, and the terrors of God terrified me
6.5: Does a wild donkey sniff over new grass or a bull cry over hay?
6.6: Or is a tasteless thing eaten without salt, or is there flavor in the mucilage of a mallows plant?
6.7: My soul was wearied in its trouble. Oh, wailing like a drunkard came to me!
6.8: Who will grant me that my request may come and God would grant my hope
6.9: And God would be persuaded and would cleanse me and would stretch out his hand and he would perfect me?
6.10: And he would again comfort me and I would be perfected again in power without sparing, because I have not been false with The Word of The Holy One
6.11: And how great is my power that I may endure, and what is my end that I should be patient in my spirit?
6.12: Oh, that my power were as the power of stone, or my flesh were of brass!
6.13: Now his help is not in me, and his salvation stays far away from me
6.14: He who withholds peace from his neighbor forsakes the worship of The Exalted One
6.15: My brothers were unreliable as brooks and like torrents that pass away
6.16: Those who have been afraid of ice, much snow has multiplied upon them!
6.17: And in an hour that shines on them they melt, and by their heat they melt from their place
6.18: The paths of their ways will be twisted, and they will lead on in a trackless waste and be destroyed
6.19: For they looked to the roads of the south and they waited for the paths of Sheba
6.20: They were ashamed in the thing that they trusted; they came to it and were ashamed
6.21: Also you have turned against me, because before you saw the earthquake you were afraid
6.22: For if had I said to you: “Give to me”, or “From your wealth offer a bribe for my sake
6.23: And deliver me from the hands of my oppressors”, or “Save me from the hand of a mighty man”
6.24: Teach me, and I shall be silent, and make me know what I have done wrong
6.25: Why do you reject the Word of truth, and who is it of you that humbles and rebukes?
6.26: Behold you will plan to correct words and you will consider my word against my spirit
6.27: Behold, you are magnified against orphans and you grieve your friend
6.28: And now, be instructed and pay attention, and I shall speak in front of you, and I shall not lie
6.29: Repent, I beg you now, and do not be as the evil. Repent therefore and be justified
6.30: Is evil in my tongue, or is my mouth not speaking truth?

7.1: Now there is time for a person on the Earth, and like the days of a hired man are his days
7.2: And like a Servant who waits for the shade, and like a hired man who waits to finish his work
7.3: In this way I have inherited for myself worthless months, and I had countless nights of pain
7.4: If I lie down, also I say, ‘when shall I arise?’ And I stretch out the evening and lie down, and I tremble until dawn
7.5: My flesh wears worms and my body, the dust; my skin is shriveled and rotted
7.6: My days are swifter than a spider’s web and they are consumed without hope
7.7: I will remember that The Spirit is life, and you shall turn my eyes back to see the goodness
7.8: The eye that sees me will not rejoice for me, and your eyes are on me, and I am not
7.9: As a cloud disappears and departs, in this way he who has descended to Sheol does not go up
7.10: And he does not return again to his house and he is not known again to his place
7.11: Also I shall not restrain my mouth, I shall speak in the grief of my spirit and I shall narrate in the bitterness of my soul
7.12: Am I the sea or a dragon that you post a watch over me?
7.13: Because I said, ‘you will comfort me and I shall take heart from the agony of my lying down’
7.14: And behold, you have disturbed me with dreams, and with visions you have terrified me
7.15: And you picked my soul out of destruction, and my bones from death
7.16: I have lost hope for myself and I am not living to eternity. Depart from me, for my days are empty!
7.17: What is man that you would destroy him and you would set your heart upon him?
7.18: And would you visit at dawn, and at that time would you test him?
7.19: And how long will you not leave me? Will you not leave me until I swallow my spittle?
7.20: If I have sinned, what shall I do to you, The Maker of Man? Why have you appointed me that I would meet with you, and you have been a burden to me?
7.21: And how long will you not forgive my debt and remove my sins? For now I shall lie down on the dust, and you will seek me, and I am not!”

8.1: And Bildad the Shukhite answered and said
8.2: How long will you speak these things and a proud spirit fill your mouth?
8.3: Will God practice evil judgment, or the Almighty One pervert the right?
8.4: If your children sinned against him, he sent them away in their sins
8.5: And if you seek God and you will beg The Almighty
8.6: If you are innocent and upright, now he will be attentive to you, and he shall restore the dwelling of your righteousness
8.7: And your beginning will be small and your end will be very great
8.8: Ask now about the first generations and discern the search of their fathers
8.9: For we are of yesterday; we know nothing, and our days on the Earth are like a shadow
8.10: And behold, those will teach you and they will say to you, and words will go forth from their heart:
8.11: “Does a reed grow in a thirsty place or papyrus reed spring up where there is no water?
8.12: While it is in its growth it is not picked, and before all grass it dries up
8.13: So are the ways of everyone who forgets God, and the hope of the pagan will be destroyed
8.14: And his trust will be cut down, and his house is the house of the spider
8.15: He will put trust on his house and it will not stand, and he will hold firmly and it will not be established
8.16: It is as green fruit before the sun
8.17: And the waves will dash against his foundation, and he will see a house of stone
8.18: If they uproot him from his place, and he will lie to him and shall say to him: “I did not see you”
8.19: Behold, he examines all their ways, and from the dust another will sprout
8.20: God does not despise the perfect and does not hold evil doers by the hand
8.21: Until your mouth will be filled with laughter and your lips with a song
8.22: Your haters will be clothed in shame and the tent of the wicked is not

9.2: “Truly I know that it is so, and no man overcomes God
9.3: If he will desire to judge his people, not one of a thousand answers him
9.4: He is wise of heart and mighty in power; who is hardened against him and has peace?
9.5: He who removes mountains, does he not know? And he overturns them in his wrath!
9.6: He who shakes the Earth from its foundations and its inhabitants quake
9.7: He who spoke to the sun and it did not rise, and seals the surfaces of the stars
9.8: He who stretched out Heaven alone and treads on the power of the sea
9.9: He who made Pleiades and the star Aldebaran and the Giant Orion and the circuit to the south
9.10: He who made great things without limit and mighty things without number
9.11: Behold, if He passes by me, I do not see him, and if he surrounds me, I do not discern him
9.12: Behold, if he destroys, who would command with his hand and who will say to him: ‘what are you doing?’
9.13: God does not turn back his anger, and under him the helpers of many will be humbled
9.14: Also I shall answer him and I shall set my words in order before him
9.15: If I am not justified, I will not be heard, and to my Judge I shall make supplication
9.16: If I called and he answered me, still I would not believe that he heard my voice
9.17: For with power he spares me in every hair of my head, and he increases my wounds without charge
9.18: And he has not left me that my spirit may rest, because bitterness filled me, and wormwood made me drunk
9.19: If one is strong in power, he is, and if he is for judgment, who is going to appease him?
9.20: If I shall claim innocence, my mouth will condemn me. I am perfect and I am perverted
9.21: I am perfect and I do not know my soul. I have despised my life.
9.22: It is one, because of this I have said: ‘He is destroying the righteous and the wicked.’
9.23: If his rod kills suddenly, also he is mocking the senselessness of the innocent
9.24: The Earth he has given into the hand of The Wicked One and the faces of Judges are covered, but who endures his anger?
9.25: My days are faster than a runner; they flee away and they see no good
9.26: They have passed by with the many ships of an enemy like an eagle that flies upon its food
9.27: With my bitterness I have forgotten my story; if I shall leave my meditation or my words, I am grieved
9.28: And if I rest, I am afraid of every torment, because I know that you do not declare me innocent
9.29: Behold, if I will be condemned, why do you destroy me as if it were nothing?
9.30: And if I am washed in water of snow and I am cleansed in the purity of my hands
9.31: Then you will sink me into a ditch, and my garments will stay away from me
9.32: Because he is not a man like me that I would give him an answer, and we would enter as one to judgment
9.33: But, oh that there would be a reprover between us that he would lay his hand on the mouth of us both!
9.34: And he would remove his rod from me, and his terror would not shake me
9.35: I would speak and I would not be afraid of him, because I have not been against him

10.1: My soul is grieved with my life. I reasoned in my reasoning and I shall speak in the bitterness of my soul
10.2: And I shall say to God: “Do not condemn me; teach me about what it is for which you judge me
10.3: Is it not enough to you that you treat unjustly and you despise the work of your hands, and you look upon the mind of the wicked?
10.4: Are your eyes as the eyes of flesh, or are you seeing like a man sees?
10.5: Or are your days as the days of man, or your years as the days of a man
10.6: That you inquire about my debts and about my sins?
10.7: And you know that I am innocent, and there is none who is delivered from your hands
10.8: Your hands labored in me and made me, and after this you choose to condemn me and to sink me!
10.9: Remember that you have made me like clay and you return me to the dust
10.10: And you churned me like milk and you curdled me like cheese
10.11: In skin and flesh you have clothed me, and with muscles and with bones you have strengthened me
10.12: And life and peace you have made with me, and my spirit has kept your commandments
10.13: And these things you have hidden in your heart; I have known that this was in your mind
10.14: For if I have sinned, you keep watch over me, and from my sins you do not declare me innocent
10.15: If I have sinned, woe to me! And if I am justified, I shall not lift my head. I am filled with disgrace and I have seen affliction!
10.16: And if I will rise up as a lion, you will hunt me and turn like a giant against me
10.17: And you oppose your weapon against me and multiply your wrath toward me, and you spring armies against me
10.18: And why have you brought me out from the womb? I might have died and an eye would not have seen me
10.19: And I had been as if I had not been, and from the womb to the tomb they would have carried me
10.20: Few are the days of my life. Depart from me and I shall be quiet and I shall be comforted a little
10.21: Before I shall go and I shall not return again, to the land of darkness and of the shadows of death
10.22: Of wasting away as deep darkness and shadows of death, and there are no ranks of generations in it, and there is languishing as deep darkness

11.1: And Tsuphar the Namthite answered and said:
11.2: “Do not answer with a multitude of words, also no man speaking is justified with his answer
11.3: Behold, at your words the dead may be silent; you speak and there is none who restrains you, and if you mock, there is none who rebukes you
11.4: And you said that you have been led righteously and you have been pure in the eyes of your soul
11.5: However the Maker, the Giver, is God the Word, and his lips are lifted up with you.
11.6: And he was showing you the secrets of wisdom, because there are inner chambers to wisdom, and you have known that God has forgiven you your sins
11.7: Can you know the declaration of God, or can you stand at the uttermost limit of The Almighty?
11.8: Do you know the high place of Heaven or the depth of Sheol? From where do you know?
11.9: Its measure is longer than the Earth and it is broader than the sea
11.10: If he will pass by, he shall seize and he shall gather; who turns him?
11.11: For he knows when time came to be, and he sees evil and he considers
11.12: And a man who is pure is encouraged, and he who is mighty helps a man
11.13: If you have prepared your heart and you have stretched forth your hands to him
11.14: If there was evil in your hands, he was removing it far away, and evil did not camp in your tent
11.15: And then you will lift your hands and you will not be afraid of sorrow
11.16: And then you shall forget your misery, and like running water you shall be led away
11.17: And at noon the grave shall rise up, and thick darkness will be like the dawn
11.18: And you will trust that there is hope and you will sleep and you will be rested
11.19: And there is none who rouses you up. Many will seek your face
11.20: And the eyes of the wicked will be darkened and their strength will be destroyed from them, and the hope of their souls

12.2: “In truth, you are the people, and wisdom will die with you!
12.3: I also have a heart like you, and I have not been less than you who have such things as these
12.4: And there was laughter with his neighbors, and he called to God and he answered him, because he is pleased with righteous men without blemish
12.5: To remove madness and evil and to restore a disjointed foot
12.6: The tents of robbers and the trust of those who are angry with God will be removed, because there is no God in their heart
12.7: Or else ask the beast and it will teach you, and the bird of the sky and it will show you
12.8: Or tell the earth and it will teach you and the fish of the sea will tell you
12.9: Who is it that does not know all these things, that the hand of LORD JEHOVAH has made these things?
12.10: Because in his hand are the souls of everything that lives and the spirit of all flesh and man
12.11: The ear hears words and the palate tastes food
12.12: And in the elders is wisdom and in length of days is discernment
12.13: With him is wisdom and heroism; his is intelligence and understanding
12.14: Behold if He casts down, who builds up, and if he shuts up the face of a man, who opens?
12.15: If he rebukes the waters, they dry up, and if he sends them out, they overturn the land
12.16: His is power and wisdom; his is strength and salvation
12.17: He governs Kings with awe and Judges he drives mad
12.18: He brings down Kings to the gates and binds a belt on their waist
12.19: He governs the Priests with awe and he brings down mighty men
12.20: He removes speech from Eunuchs and the discretion of old men he takes away
12.21: He pours madness on Rulers and the courage of the mighty he weakens
12.22: He reveals deep things from darkness and brings out the light from the shadows of death
12.23: He deceives the nations and scatters them. He strikes down the nations and destroys them. He spreads out the peoples and he leaves them
12.24: He intoxicates the heart of the Leaders of the people of the Earth and causes them to wander in a trackless waste without a road
12.25: They will search in darkness and not in the light, and they will grope in the darkness and not the light, and he inebriates them like drunkards

13.1: Behold, my eyes saw all these things, and my ear heard and I considered
13.2: I know according to your knowledge also, and I have not been inferior to you
13.3: However, I am speaking to The Almighty One, and I shall speak my reproof to God
13.4: However, you are speaking falsehood, and you are healers of nothing
13.5: He, The Maker, is silent; you should be silent, and let this be for your wisdom
13.6: Hear, therefore, my reproof, and obey the judgment of my lips
13.7: You speak evil about God and do you speak deceit about him?
13.8: Do you accept his person, or do you judge with God?
13.9: It is good for you that he investigates you, if as a man is judged you are judged by him in all things
13.10: He shall surely reprove you if you will accept his person!
13.11: His dread is your punishment and his terror will fall on you
13.12: Remember that your power is from the dust, and your lofty dwelling is upon a bank of clay
13.13: Be silent before me and I shall speak also, and I will tell everything that has passed against me
13.14: Why do I take up my flesh into my teeth, and my soul is put into my hands?
13.15: If he will kill me, it is for him alone I look, because my ways are before him
13.16: Also he shall be to me The Savior, because a phony does not come into his presence
13.17: Listen to hear my words and I shall tell my story before you
13.18: Behold, also I bring my judgment and I know that I am innocent
13.19: Who is he that contends with me? For now I shall be silent and be at rest
13.20: However, two things do not turn away from me, and then I shall not turn aside from before you
13.21: Do not remove your hand from me, and do not let your awesomeness terrify me
13.22: Call me and I shall answer, or I shall speak and bring me an answer
13.23: How many debts and sins do I have? My debts and my sins teach me
13.24: Why do you turn your face from me and you consider me as an enemy to you?
13.25: Do you trample a dry leaf that falls, or do you chase a dry blade of grass?
13.26: For you have written instruction against me, and you remind me of the sin debts of my youth
13.27: And you set my feet in the stocks and have set a guard on all my ways, and shall you look upon the footprints of my feet?
13.28: And he is as a skin which wears out, and like clothing which a moth eats.

14.1: Thus is the man born of woman, and he is few of days and suffers terror
14.2: And like a flower he comes forth and dries up and fades and flees as a shadow and does not remain
14.3: Also on this one you opened your eyes, and will you bring him into judgment with you?
14.4: Who will render the pure from the defiled? Not even one!
14.5: If his days are cut off and the number of his months are circumcised, you have made the Law that he will not pass from it
14.6: And he will breathe until his days run out as a hired man
14.7: Because there is hope for a tree that if it is cut, it is renewed again; its offshoots are not lacking
14.8: And if its root grows old in the earth and its vegetation may die in the dust
14.9: From the scent of waters it will flourish and it will make leaves as a plant
14.10: And a man dies and wastes away and a son of man is finished and he is not
14.11: Waters disappear from the sea, and a river dwindles and dries up
14.12: And a man who falls asleep does not rise until the Heavens wear out; they will not be awakened and they do not break out from their sleep
14.13: But The Maker who is in Sheol has hidden me and concealed me until your wrath had passed, but you set a resurrection for me and you remembered me
14.14: If a man dies does he live? And all the days of his youth he waits until his old age comes
14.15: If you call me I shall answer you, and upon the work of your hands I shall think
14.16: Because now, do not number my goings and do not hold my sins against me
14.17: Seal my debts in a bundle and remove my sins from me
14.18: Truly a mighty mountain falls or a precipice is being shaken from its place
14.19: The waters wear away stone or a clod of the dust of the Earth. You have destroyed the hope of man
14.20: You will prevail over him to eternity and he will walk in the shame of his face; you will cast him away
14.21: If his sons will be multiplied, he does not know, and if they diminish, it is not known to them
14.22: However his flesh upon him will sorrow, and his soul will mourn for him”

15.1: And Eliphaz the Taymanite answered and said:
15.2: “Will the wise of spirit answer in knowledge and anger fill his belly
15.3: To conceal words that are not useful and with words without profit in them?
15.4: Also you bring reverence to nothing and you multiply speaking before God
15.5: Because sin will teach your mouth and you delight in a tongue of deceit
15.6: Your mouth will condemn you and not I, and your lips will testify against you before me
15.7: Were you born before man or were you formed in the womb before the hills?
15.8: Have you heard the secret of LORD JEHOVAH, and is wisdom revealed to you?
15.9: What do you know that we do not know, or what do you discern that was not with us?
15.10: Behold, there are with us those who are old, also are with us those who are old and more ancient also than your father in days
15.11: Restrain from yourself the threatening of God and speak in comfort to your soul
15.12: Why is your heart lifted up and why do your eyes beckon us?
15.13: For you are lifting up your spirit to God and you bring out words from your mouth
15.14: Who is the man who is innocent, or who born of woman is going to be justified?
15.15: Behold, in his holy ones he does not trust, and the Heavens are not pure before him
15.16: Even if, rejected and afflicted, a man will drink evil as water
15.17: I shall show you, hear me, and what I have seen I shall repeat
15.18: What the wise have shown and have not hidden from their fathers
15.19: To them by themselves the Earth was given and no stranger passed among them
15.20: All the days of the wicked he magnifies himself and the number of his years will be kept by The Almighty One
15.21: The sound of terror is in his ears and in peace the Destroyer will come upon him
15.22: He does not believe that he is returning from darkness. He looks for the sword
15.23: He flees for the threat of judgment and wherever he is, he knows that the day of darkness is prepared at his hand
15.24: Suffering will terrify him and adversity will overtake him as a King who is prepared for battle
15.25: Because he lifted up his hand against God and was valiant against The Almighty
15.26: And he runs upon him with a high neck, and on the back of his neck with a multitude of rows of shields
15.27: And he covered his person with his fat and made Pleiades higher than the star Aldebaran
15.28: And he settled desolate cities that are uninhabited and is prepared for battle
15.29: He will not continue and his power shall not stand, and the words not will be exalted on the Earth
15.30: And they shall not depart from darkness and the flames will dry up his branches; he will be made insane by the spirit of his mouth
15.31: And he should not believe the lie of the Deceiver, because his growth was in vain
15.32: And because he does not dry up in his days and his hands are not found
15.33: He will uproot his body like a vine and will cast off his growth like an olive tree
15.34: Because the assembly of the heathen will be for the wilderness and for desolation, and fire will consume the tabernacle of the evil
15.35: They conceived evil and gave birth to depravity and their bellies are filled with deceit”

16.2: “I have heard of many things like these, and you are comforters in evil, all of you!
16.3: Do not grieve my spirit with words. Even though you will speak to me, I am not answering
16.4: I also might speak like you, but, oh that your souls were treated in the place of my soul, and I had tested you with words and I would shake my head at you!
16.5: But I would have examined you in your words, and the speaking of my lips I would not have kept from you!
16.6: For if I shall speak, my sorrow is not removed, and I am if silent, who is relieving me?
16.7: Because now he troubles me and he keeps all my testimony
16.8: And he appointed me and I have become a witness and my lie stood against me, and I have spoken before him
16.9: His anger has broken me and torn me to pieces and his teeth gnashed on me. My enemies fixed their eyes on me
16.10: And they have opened their mouths against me with insult and they struck my cheeks together, and they have been filled with fury against me
16.11: He handed me over to the Evil Angel, and he handed me over to the hand of the Wicked One
16.12: I was silent and he struck me. He seized on my neck and shook me. He has set me up for himself as a target
16.13: And his arrows have increased against me, and he shot at my kidneys and showed no pity. He poured my bile on the ground!
16.14: He broke me breach within breach and he ran against me as upon a giant!
16.15: Sackcloth I put on my skin and I sprinkled my head with dust
16.16: My face is troubled from weeping and my eyes from the shadow of death
16.17: Because there is no evil in my hands and my prayer is pure
16.18: The Earth will not cover my blood and there shall be no place for my cry
16.19: And now, behold, in Heaven are my witnesses, and my acquaintances in the High Place
16.20: My brothers and my friends, my eyes overflow to God!
16.21: But, oh, that a son of man might blame God, as a man his neighbor!
16.22: For the number of his years are coming, and he goes the way without return!

17.1: My spirit is exhausted and my days have become extinct! The tomb is ready for me!
17.2: Because there is no falsehood with me, and in their bitterness my spirit spends the night
17.3: Set now my tent with you! Why is this entrusted into my hands?
17.4: Because you have hidden their heart from understanding, therefore they shall be exalted in their deception
17.5: And a friend will be lifted up over his friend, also the eyes of their sons will be darkened
17.6: He raised up the Ruler of the nations, and he created a veil over their faces
17.7: My eye aches from passion and all my senses are as a shadow
17.8: The upright will be amazed for this, and the innocent one will be emptied out for the guilty
17.9: The righteous shall hold his way and will grasp firmly, and he whose hands are clean will add to power
17.10: However, behold, all of you returned and you turned and you came to me, and I did not find among you one who is wise
17.11: Passers of days! Aborters of ideas! Destroyers of the hearts of people!
17.12: You put the night for day, and you sacrifice the light to the darkness
17.13: If I shall lie in ambush, my house is in Sheol, and I deserve my bed in darkness
17.14: To destruction I cried to the worm: ‘you are my father!’, and ‘my mother and my sister!’
17.15: ‘Where is my hope and my trust from now on? And my hope, who shall find it?
17.16: Into the bottom to Sheol you will go down!’ And together to the dust they shall descend!”

18.1: And Beldad the Shukhite answered and said:
18.2: “How long will you put up opposition to words? Discern, and in the end we shall speak!
18.3: Why are we considered as a beast and we are defiled in your eyes?
18.4: He kills his soul in his wrath. Behold, because of you shall the Earth be forsaken, and a mountain move from its place?
18.5: Also the lamp of the wicked will be extinguished; also the flame of its fire will be put out.
18.6: The light will darken in their tents and its lamp will be extinguished over him
18.7: His steps will be laid waste in his sickness and his thoughts will sink him
18.8: Because he stretched his feet into a snare and he has walked upon a net
18.9: And snares grasped on his heel; droughts will prevail over him
18.10: His destruction is hidden in the earth and his snare shall wrap around on the path
18.11: Rows of terror surrounded him and they scattered themselves at his feet
18.12: And his affliction will be a famine, and ruin is prepared for his posterity
18.13: His possessions will be devoured by violence, and his first born dies by violence
18.14: His trust will be cut off from his tent and the terrors of Kings will terrify him
18.15: And they shall dwell in his tent, that which no man has; brimstone will be cast on his place
18.16: His roots will dry up from beneath and his vintage will fall off from above
18.17: His memorial will be destroyed from the Earth, and he has no name on the face of creation
18.18: He will be thrown out from light into darkness and from the world they will reject him
18.19: He has no name and no memory among the people and he has no survivor in his dwelling
18.20: Let the latter ones be stupefied at his days, and the hair of the first ones stands on end
18.21: However these are the tents of the evil, and this is the place of him who does not know God

19.2: “How long will you grieve my soul and afflict me with words?
19.3: Behold, ten times you have rebuked me and you are unashamed that you sadden me
19.4: And if truly I have gone astray, my error will be with me
19.5: And if, from truth, you have magnified yourselves against me, and you have rebuked me and reproached me
19.6: Know therefore that God is my creditor, and he has spread his net over me
19.7: And if I wail, no man answers me, and if I shall make an appeal, there is none who redeems me
19.8: My ways he has fenced in that I may not pass, and laid darkness on my path
19.9: My glory he stripped from me, and has taken away the crown of my head
19.10: He has uprooted me from all my sides and I am gone, and he picked up my hope like wood
19.11: And his anger had grown strong against me, and he considers me as an enemy to him
19.12: And his Apostles together came against me and they disputed their ways against me and they camped around my tent
19.13: My brothers have gone far from me and my acquaintances have left
19.14: My neighbors are cut off from my presence and my acquaintances have forgotten me
19.15: Dwellers in my house and my maids consider me as a foreigner, and I am a stranger in their eyes
19.16: I called my Servant and he did not answer me, and I entreated him with the words of my mouth and I begged him
19.17: I have been a stranger to my wife and I have begged the son of my bowels
19.18: Also the evil abhorred me; I stood up and they spoke against me
19.19: All the counselors of my counsel have despised me, also my friends turned against me
19.20: My skin and my flesh is stuck on my bones and I have escaped by the skin of my teeth
19.21: Have pity on me! Have pity on me, you my friends, because it is the hand of God that has touched me!
19.22: Why will you persecute me, even you, like God, and with my flesh you will not be satisfied?
19.23: But, oh Creator, that my words were written and inscribed in a scroll
19.24: And with a pen of iron and with a pen of lead for eternity, and upon stone they would be inscribed!
19.25: And I know that my Redeemer is living, and in the end on the Earth he shall be revealed
19.26: And over my skin it is I will be surrounded with these things and over my flesh
19.27: If my eyes will see God, my kidneys see the light. It is ending, for I am ended by my resurrection
19.28: For you say: ‘why do we persecute him?’ For The Good Word has found occasion against me
19.29: Save yourselves from before the sword, because the wrath of sins is a sword, so that you will know there is a judgment!”

20.1: And Tsuphar the Namthite answered and said:
20.2: “Because my goings were discerned, and for my sake they persisted for me
20.3: The instruction of my reproof I shall hear, and the spirit of my understanding will answer me
20.4: This I have known from the world from when Adam was created on the Earth, from The Maker of man on the Earth
20.5: Because the boasting of the wicked from within them, and the rejoicing of the godless one until now is for a little time
20.6: If his height ascends to Heaven and his head will arrive to the clouds
20.7: As a hurricane he will be destroyed for eternity; those who saw him will say: ‘Where is he?’
20.8: And like a dream he will fly and will not be found, and he will flee as a vision of the night
20.9: The eye that saw him will not see him again, and they will consider his place no more
20.10: His sons will be broken in poverty and his hands he will reach out to his child
20.11: His bones are filled with marrow and will lie down with him on the Earth
20.12: If his evil will be sweet in his mouth and he will hide deceit under his tongue
20.13: He will show sympathy for it and he will not forsake it or restrain it within his darkness
20.14: His bread in his stomach will be turned, and the venom of The Dragon is within him
20.15: And the food that he swallowed he will vomit from his belly and God will destroy him
20.16: The venom of the asp he will pour out and the tongue of an asp will kill him
20.17: He will not see the tributaries of rivers, and of torrents of honey and of butter
20.18: He will return to labor and he will not swallow with strength; his barter he shall not enjoy
20.19: For he thought to forsake the poor, and a house of violence will not be built up
20.20: Because he did not know his judgment in his gut, and by his lusts he will not escape
20.21: There is none who escapes from his generations, therefore his good will not remain
20.22: In the measure that he measured he shall be paid, and every hand of the evil will come upon him
20.23: And his conception will be for destruction, and the heat of his anger will be prolonged in him and it will rain upon him with its warlike strength
20.24: For he fled from breastplates of iron, a bow of brass will shoot through him
20.25: It is drawn and it brings out his insides and the skin of his gall will be pierced; truly power is against him
20.26: And all darkness is hidden for his generations; fire will consume him that he will not breathe, and a survivor will do evil in his tabernacle
20.27: Heaven will reveal his sins and Earth will be avenged on him
20.28: The foundations of his house will be exposed, and he will be scourged in the day of rage
20.29: This is the portion of a wicked son of man from before God, and the inheritance that will fill him from The Highest”

21.2: “Listen to hear my words and it will be understanding among you
21.3: Be patient with me and I shall speak, and after my words, mock on
21.4: I shall speak my words to a son of man: ‘why is my spirit distressed?
21.5: Return to me and marvel, and put your hands on your mouth!
21.6: For if I remembered I was disturbed, and terror seized my flesh
21.7: Because, why are the wicked alive and mighty men of power growing old?
21.8: Their seed are established before them, and their children in their sight
21.9: Their households are quieted of fear and there is no rod of God upon them
21.10: His ox breeds and does not bellow, and his cow brings forth and it is not destroyed
21.11: Their sons are firm as a flock, and their children dance
21.12: They picked up tambourines and harp and rejoiced to the voice of singing
21.13: They spend their days in prosperity, and quickly to Sheol they descend
21.14: And they were saying to God: ‘Depart from us’, and ‘We do not want to know your ways!’
21.15: And they were saying: ‘Who is God that we would serve him, or what do we gain that we should seek him?’
21.16: Behold, their benefit was not by their hands, and the thinking of the wicked is far from me
21.17: How often the lamps of the wicked will be extinguished, and ruin will come upon them! He will distribute corruption in his anger!
21.18: And they shall be as straw before the wind and like chaff that a tornado picks up!
21.19: God keeps his evil for his children and he will pay him and he will know
21.20: His eyes will see his destruction, and he will drink from the anger of God
21.21: What is the pleasure in his house after him, and the number of his months kept for his children?
21.22: Do you teach God knowledge? For he will judge the proud!
21.23: For if this one should die in the strength of full age, he is trusting and quiet
21.24: And his sides are filled with fat, and his bones are filled with marrow
21.25: That one will die in a soul of bitterness and not eat in prosperity
21.26: They lie down together on the Earth, and worms will cover them
21.27: Behold, I know your thoughts and the counsels that you are counseling about me
21.28: Because you have said: ‘Where is the house of the righteous one, and what is the dwelling of the tent of the wicked?’
21.29: For have you not asked passersby of the road, and have you not perceived their signs?
21.30: Because the evil is kept for the day of destruction and he is remembered for the day of rage
21.31: Who is showing him the way, and who is paying him what he did?
21.32: But he goes to the tomb and is kept for an accident
21.33: Deep caves of a torrent swallow him, and every person is dragged after him and before him without number
21.34: And how will you comfort me with futility, and have the answers of the reprobate given profit before me?”

22.1: And Eliphaz the Taymanite answered, and he said:
22.2: “Do you, a man, speak with God , that you are equal with him in wisdom?
22.3: And what is the gain that you perfect your ways from your fear?
22.4: He will rebuke you and will enter into judgment with you
22.5: Behold, your evil is great and there is no end to your sin
22.6: Because you took the tent of your brothers for nothing and you stripped the garments of the naked
22.7: You gave no water for the weary to drink and from the hungry you have withheld bread
22.8: There is a man who sows the land for himself , and a lord will take it from him by force
22.9: A widow you have sent away with nothing and the arm of the orphan was brought low
22.10: Because of this, snares surround you and fear shakes you suddenly
22.11: From where the darkness is, you have not seen, and pouring forth of waters will cover you
22.12: Behold, God lifts up Heaven and he sees the top stars that are exalted
22.13: And you said: ‘What does God know? Will he judge within the thick darkness?’
22.14: Clouds are for his shelter and they do not see, and upon the orb of Heaven he walks
22.15: Have you kept the way of the world that the people of lying trod that was not in their time?
22.16: And at crossing of the river they were stopped and they did not remember him who laid their foundations
22.17: And they were saying to God: ‘depart from us, and we do not want to know your ways, and what does God do for us?’
22.18: And he fills their households with blessing and the scheming of the evil was kept far from them
22.19: The righteous shall see and they will rejoice and the innocent ones will mock at them
22.20: But they will be humbled from their hardness and fire will consume their remnant
22.21: But make an agreement with him and hand over some of your good crops
22.22: Accept the law from his mouth and he shall put his words in your heart
22.23: If you are converted to God, you will be built up, and if you will put evil far from your dwelling
22.24: And you will accumulate silver as the dust, and the gold of Ophir like the sand of the valley
22.25: And God will be for your help and you shall have accounting money
22.26: And you will lift your face to God
22.27: And you will pray before him and he will hear you and you will hand over your vows
22.28: And you will speak a word and it will happen to you, and upon your ways the light will dawn
22.29: Because he who is humbled, he says that he will be lifted up, and he whose his eyes are meek shall be redeemed
22.30: The innocent will escape where he is, and he will escape by the purity of his hands”

23.2: “Also today my tale is bitter and his hand has been heavy on my groaning
23.3: But who makes it possible for me to arrive unto his seat?
23.4: And I shall set judgment in order before him and I shall fill my mouth with rebuke
23.5: And I had known what he answered me and I had understood what he said to me
23.6: In the abundance of power he judged with me, and otherwise there was awe laid upon me
23.7: There I would have judged justly with him, and I would have been justified, for I would have been allowed to escape in judgment
23.8: If he goes before me I do not know, or from behind him I do not perceive him
23.9: I looked at my left and he did not appear to me, and I turned to my right and I did not see him
23.10: He has known my way and my existence and he has tested me as gold, and I came forth
23.11: And my feet were established in his steps; his ways I have kept
23.12: And I have not despised the commands of his lips, and I have not departed from his pleasure. I have kept the words of his mouth
23.13: And in what one of these will he return to me, for his soul delights and he does it?
23.14: However, he completed his covenant, and there are many things like these with him
23.15: Because of this, I am afraid before him, I consider and I am shaken by him
23.16: Because God has shaken my heart and my head is shaken
23.17: Because I was not silenced by the presence of the darkness and by the presence of the veil of gloom

24.1: Why do the evil not hide themselves from the presence of God, and they are famous and they have not seen their days?
24.2: And the border they have removed and the flock they have taken
24.3: And male donkeys of orphans they have taken and they pawn the ox of a widow
24.4: The evil have hidden themselves by the road, and the meek of the Earth hide themselves together
24.5: As wild donkeys in the wilderness that rise early for food, for bread for their young
24.6: And in the field hay that is not theirs they will reap, and the vineyard of the wicked they will gather
24.7: Spending the night naked for lack of clothing and without a cloak in the cold
24.8: From the downpour of the mountains they will be wet and from lack of refuge, they will embrace stones
24.9: They seize some pillage of orphans, and on the poor they set desolations
24.10: Naked they walked for lack of clothing and the hungry picked up bread
24.11: And in the house of banquets they stoop and are hungry while carrying a basket and a measure, and in time they will hunger in their feasts; treading they will tread and be thirsty
24.12: From within the city the dying will groan, and the soul of the slain will cry out, and God will not receive their prayer
24.13: Those have been in lofty dwellings of light and they did not know his ways, and they did not walk in his steps
24.14: In the light stands a killer and he kills the poor and the afflicted and is as a thief by night
24.15: And eyes of adulterers watch for darkness and they say: “Eyes will not see”, and he conceals his face in darkness
24.16: He breaks into houses in darkness and by day marks them so that they will not see the light
24.17: Therefore they sought for themselves the shadow of death, and they found it, and the shadows of death terrified them
24.18: Quickly and upon the face of the waters will be carried away their portion in the earth, in the road of the vine dressers
24.19: Drought and heat will seize the waters of the snow, into Sheol they have sinned
24.20: And they were forgotten from the womb; the worm sucked them and the dead will not be remembered again, and the evil one will be broken like wood
24.21: The barren evil woman shall not bear; they have not done good to the widow
24.22: And the property of a mighty one shall stand by his power, and he will not believe in a vision
24.23: And he will dwell in quietness and his eyes will be confident also on their ways
24.24: They will be lifted up a little and they are not, and they will be humbled and all the provokers will be destroyed, and like the head of blades of wheat they dry out and will be crumbled
24.25: And if it was not his anger, he will convict me of lying, and my word shall be considered before God”

25.1: And Beldad the Shukhite answered and said:
25.2: “Authority and awe are with him. He made peace in his high places
25.3: And there is no computation to his power, and upon whom does his light not dawn?
25.4: And how can a son of man be justified by God, or how is one born of a woman righteous?
25.5: Behold, the moon cannot be pure and the stars are not pure before him
25.6: But truly man is a worm and a son of man is a maggot”

26.2: “Why are you a helper without power, and have you saved the arm without strength?
26.3: How have you counseled him who is without wisdom, and how much instruction have you made known?
26.4: To whom have you shown words and whose soul has proceeded from you?
26.5: Behold the Giants shall be killed and will be quieter than the waters
26.6: Sheol is naked before him and there is no covering for destruction
26.7: He has stretched out the North from the waste place and has suspended the Earth on nothing
26.8: He has bound the waters in his clouds and a cloud does not burst under them
26.9: He has seized on the face of the covering and spread a cloud over it
26.10: He drew a circle on the face of the waters, unto the ends of the light with the darkness
26.11: The pillars of Heaven shake and shall be astonished at his rebuke
26.12: By his power he rebukes the sea and in his wisdom he gives life to the multitude
26.13: By his Spirit he governs Heaven, and his hand killed the Serpent which fled
26.14: Behold these all are from the tip of his ways, and what evil statement has been heard about him, and the immensity of his heroism who comprehends?

27.1: And Job added to speak his parable and said:
27.2: “Living is God who has twisted up my judgment and The Almighty who spoke to my soul
27.3: “All of whom is in me and The Spirit of God is in my nostrils
27.4: My lips will not speak evil and my tongue will not speak deceit
27.5: God forbid me until I shall die, my integrity will not depart from me!
27.6: And I shall hold fast to my righteousness and I shall not let it go, and my heart does not chastise me from my days
27.7: And my enemies will be as the wicked and those who hate me, as the evil
27.8: What is the hope of the heathen who possesses riches at the time when God takes his soul?
27.9: Because God does not hear the voice of his prayer when evil will come upon him
27.10: But if he will trust on The Almighty and he will cry out to God at all times, God will answer him and will listen to him
27.11: But I deliver you into the hand of God that your works are not hidden from him
27.12: Behold, all of you have seen, and why are you magnifying yourselves for nothing?
27.13: Behold, this is the portion of a wicked son of man from the presence of God, and they shall receive the inheritance of evildoers that is from The Almighty One
27.14: If their children will be multiplied for the sword and their offspring, they will not be filled with bread
27.15: And their survivors will be buried in death and their widows will not weep
27.16: If they will gather money like dust, and would prepare clothing like clay
27.17: Those will prepare and the righteous one will wear and he will divide their money
27.18: For he builds his house like spiders, and he made his tabernacle like a booth
27.19: Because the rich will lie down and he will not be rising again; he opens his eyes, and he is not
27.20: Trouble will overtake him like waters and The Spirit in the night like a flying tempest will take him away
27.21: And he will go and it will take him away from his place
27.22: And he will cast him out without pity, and by his hand he puts to flight, and he will scatter
27.23: His hand will strike upon them and he will hiss at them from his place

28.1: Because there is silver coming forth and the place where gold pours out
28.2: And iron is melted from the dust, and from the rocks copper is melted
28.3: He set an end for darkness and he knows the end to everything, the stone of a deep pit and the shadow of death
28.4: A mountain pass they inherited from alien people; they have strayed from the torrents and are cut off from man
28.5: They lay bare the Earth from which food came forth, and under it is turned up as fire
28.6: And the place of sapphire, its stone, and the pouring out of gold in its paths
28.7: A bird of prey has not known it, and the eye of the vulture has not seen it
28.8: And animals have not trodden it, and the lion does not cross over it
28.9: His hand reaches on the rock of flints and overturns the mountains from their roots
28.10: In his might he divides rivers and his eye has seen every precious thing
28.11: And the strength of rivers he had shut in, and from the veil went out light
28.12: And where shall his wisdom be found, and what is the place of understanding?
28.13: No man knows her treasury, and it is not found except in the place of life
28.14: The depths said: ‘It was not in me!”, and the sea said: ‘It is not with me!’
28.15: Gold is not given in exchange for it, neither is silver counted for its price
28.16: It is not exchanged in gold of Ophir, or in beryl, or in sapphire
28.17: Gold does not compare to it, or crystal, or precious pearls, neither do gems or jewels compare to it
28.18: Better is wisdom than precious stones; agate does not compare to it, neither with emeralds nor with chalcedony is it exchanged, for wisdom is better than everything, and nothing compares to it
28.19: The pearls of KushEthiopia and the stones of the ephod are not comparable to it
28.20: From where comes wisdom, and what is the place of understanding?
28.21: It is covered to the eyes of everything that lives, and from the bird of the sky it is hidden
28.22: Destruction and death said: ‘we have heard its report with our ears’
28.23: Because God is discerning its ways and he knows its place
28.24: Because he sees the end of everything that is in the Earth, and he sees under all Heaven
28.25: Because he made a scale for the wind and has prepared a weight with a measure
28.26: He made a law for the rain and a way for the sights of loud noise
28.27: Then he saw it and he told it, he weighed it, also he examined it
28.28: And he said to a son of man: ‘the awe of God is wisdom, and to depart from evil is understanding’”

29.1: And Job continued to tell his parable, and said:
29.2: “But who will make me as the first months and like the days when God preserved me?
29.3: His awe he made above my head in spreading his lamp over me, and by his light I walked in the dark
29.4: As I was in the days of my favor when God was resting over my tent
29.5: And when The Almighty was surrounding my youth
29.7: When I proceeded to the gate and I called, and I sat in the market as the afflicted
29.8: Young men saw me and they hid themselves, and the Elders stood and were amazed at me
29.9: And Princes were restrained in words, and they placed their hands on their mouths
29.10: The voices of Leaders were concealed, and their tongue stuck to the roof of their jaws
29.11: Because the ear that heard praised me, and he whom I saw testified to me
29.12: Because I saved the poor from suffering, and the orphan who had no helper
29.13: The blessing of the perishing would enter upon me, and the heart of the widow was gladdened
29.14: I wore truth and it clothed me as a mantle and a crown of judgment
29.15: I have been eyes to the blind and feet to the crippled
29.16: I was a father to the afflicted, and judgment that I had not known I searched out
29.17: I have broken the molars of the evil man and I took the prey from between his teeth
29.18: And I said to the poor people: ‘I shall deliver’, and I shall end like a reed stalk, and like the sand of the seas I shall multiply my days
29.19: My roots are planted by the waters and the dew will stay, and in harvest I shall be invited
29.20: It was held with me, also my bow is renewed in my hands
29.21: And if they heard me and they hoped and they heard my advice
29.22: And from my words they have not erred and my word was pleasing to them
29.23: They waited for me as for the rain and their mouths they opened wide as for spring rain
29.24: I shall laugh at them and they will not believe, and in the light of my face they will not walk
29.25: I shall search their ways and I shall return and I shall dwell as a King among his army and like a man who comforts mourners

30.1: And now, behold, those that are inferior to me have laughed at me
30.2: In those days that I despised their fathers and I did not value them with the dogs of my flocks
30.3: And the power of their hands, why did it go to me?
30.4: For all strength was lost upon them
30.5: And with force he will seek for them like a thief with the strength of a torrent
30.6: To dwell in caverns of dust and stones
30.7: And under cliffs and under thorn bushes
30.8: The foolish children will be overthrown with evil children; there they will be leveled from the Earth
30.9: And now I have been their meditation, and I have been a horror to them
30.10: They despise me and are removed far from me and they did not restrain spitting from my face
30.11: Because they started after me and they humiliated me, and they put a bridle in my mouth
30.12: On my right they stood up and they tripped up my feet and they confused me in the path of their ways
30.13: And they entangled my steps for nothing, and they rejoiced for whatever happened to me, and they will have no helper
30.14: As a great ruin will come, and they will be broken under a hurricane
30.15: For they turned trouble against me and pursued my path like the wind, and my salvation is passing like a cloud
30.16: And now my soul is troubled, and the days of my affliction have surrounded me
30.17: In the night my bones weigh upon me, and my body does not rest in an abundance of power
30.18: I am clothed with garments and I am girded in my tunic
30.19: They likened me to mud and I am compared to dust and ashes
30.20: I shall cry to you and you will not answer me; I stood and you noticed me
30.21: And you have made me an enemy to you, and with the strength of your hands you have bound me
30.22: And you have picked me up and you caused me to lie down on the wind, and you afflicted me and you made me wretched
30.23: Therefore I know that you will return me from death to the house and the meeting of All Life
30.24: However, he will not reach his hand against me, and whenever I have cried out to him, he will save me
30.25: I wept for the poor by day, and my soul was troubled for the afflicted one
30.26: I have waited for good and evil came; I have waited for the light, and blackness came
30.27: My bowels were alarmed and did not cease, the days of my affliction went before me
30.28: I have walked sadly without anger, I stood in the assembly and I wept
30.29: I have been a brother to jackals, and a friend to the daughters of ostriches
30.30: My skin is shrunken against me, and my bones are warped as if by parching heat
30.31: My harp was for grieving, and my song for the voice of weeping

31.1: I have established a covenant for my eyes that I shall not take notice of a virgin
31.2: And what is the portion of God from above, and the inheritance of God from the high place
31.3: But ruin to the evil and alienation upon the Servants of evil?
31.4: He sees my ways and he counts all my goings
31.5: If I have walked with deceivers and if my feet hasten to run in treachery
31.6: He will weigh me in a balance of truth and God shall know my integrity
31.7: If I have turned my goings away from the road, and if my heart goes after my eyes, and if anything is stuck in me
31.8: But if I have sown then I ate, and if I planted, then I raised and I stored up
31.9: And if my heart was enticed by an estranged woman, or if I lay in wait at the door of my neighbor
31.10: Unless my wife has ground meal for others and she has baked in another place
31.11: Because this is sin, and this is an eye that is of contrivance
31.12: And it is fire that consumes unto destruction, and all my crops would be uprooted
31.13: If I have despised the justice of my Servants or of my Maidservants when they have been contending with me
31.14: But I have said, ‘what shall I do when God stands up and when he commands, what I shall return to him?’
31.15: Behold, in the womb The One who made me made him, and in one womb he formed us
31.16: If I have withheld from the poor a thing that he desired, or if I have darkened the eyes of a widow
31.17: If I ate my bread alone and the orphan did not eat of it
31.18: Because sorrows from my youth raised me and groanings from the belly of my mother
31.19: If I have seen one lost who was without clothing, and the afflicted without a cloak
31.20: If they were not reared on my knees or warmed from the wool of my sheep
31.21: If I have lifted my hands against the orphan, (but whenever I saw him at the door I helped him)
31.22: My shoulder will fall from its socket and my arm will be broken from its shoulder
31.23: Because the awe of God shook me and his ruin came against me, and I could not stand from terror
31.24: If I have made gold my trust and if I have said to precious stones: ‘you are my trust’, and if I have said to pure gold, ‘you are my hope’
31.25: And if I was rejoicing when my property increased and when my hands found much
31.26: If I have seen light when peaceful and the moon when shining
31.27: If I go and my heart is enticed in secret and I have kissed my hands with my mouth
31.28: Also he sees all my devices, if I have lied before God
31.29: And if I am gladdened in the ruin of my hater or if I have celebrated when evil happened to him
31.30: And I have not yielded my mouth to sin and my soul has not asked for one of these things
31.31: But my friends said: ‘but who gave us of his flesh and we are not satisfied!’
31.32: I have not made a stranger lodge in the street and I opened my door to the wayfarer
31.33: If I have hidden my sins as a man and if I have hid my debts in a secret place
31.34: And if I have trampled the power of many, (but it is an abundance of families that ruined me) and a son of man I have not turned away at the gate, and I have not ruminated on the speech of the lips) the provocations of God have humbled me
31.35: But The Giver who hears me, if he is God, he will answer me, and he will write in a scroll his judgments of a man
31.36: But on my shoulder, I would take it up and I would have made it a crown for myself
31.37: I shall show him the number of my steps, and like a Ruler I would declare it
31.38: And the Earth will wail over me and its furrows will weep as one
31.39: If I consumed its power without expense, or if I have made the soul of the bitter drunk
31.40: Instead of wheat will come forth a dung heap, and instead of barley will come forth thorns.” And the statements of Job are finished

32.1: And these three men who had wanted the condemnation of Job were silent, because he was righteous in their eyes
32.2: And Elihu son of Barakiel, the Buzite, from the generation of Ramun, his anger was provoked against Job, for he justified himself more than God
32.3: And he was provoked against his three fellows because they were not able to give an answer to Job and to condemn him
32.4: And Elihu rebuked Job with words, because they were older than he in days
32.5: And Elihu saw that these three men were unable to give him an answer, and his anger was provoked
32.6: And Elihu, son of Barakiel the Buzite answered, and he said: “Because I am lesser than you in days, and you are Elders, because of this I was afraid and I was shaken to show you my knowledge
32.7: For I have said, ‘days are speaking and a multitude of years teach wisdom’
32.8: Truly a spirit is in a man and the breath of God is discernment to them
32.9: It is not therefore abundance of days that makes wise, neither is it the old ones who discern judgment
32.10: Because of this, I say: ‘hear me, and also I shall show you my knowledge’
32.11: Behold, I kept silent for you and for your words, and I paid attention until you finished and I examined you with words
32.12: And I discerned your testimony, and behold, there is no reprover for Job, or one of you giving him an answer
32.13: For you say, ‘we have found wisdom; God is he who has wounded him, and not a son of man’
32.14: I will not speak contradictory words, and I do not offer him your counsel”
32.15: They stopped and they did not answer him; they abstained from words and they kept silent
32.16: Because they were not speaking, therefore they rose up and they did not answer him again
32.17: “I shall answer also with my word, and I shall show also my knowledge
32.18: Because I am filled with words and The Spirit is poured out into my belly
32.19: Behold, my belly is pained, and is not opened, and like Ab – in the month of harvest, it bursts
32.20: I shall speak and it will relieve me; I shall open my lips and I shall give an answer
32.21: I shall not accept the face of a man, and I shall not be ashamed of the face of a man
32.22: Because I do not know shame, or in a little while my Strength would have taken me away

33.1: However, hear Job, my words, and give ear to all my words
33.2: Behold, I have opened my mouth and in my mouth my tongue speaks
33.3: Upright is the speaking of my mouth and chosen is the speech of my lips
33.4: The Spirit of God has awakened me and the breath of God has given me life
33.5: If can you return an answer, be ready and stand before me
33.6: I, like you, am with God, also I was formed from clay
33.7: And now my dread will not shake you and my anxiety will not be a burden for you
33.8: However, you have spoken in my ear and the voice of your words I have heard
33.9: For you have said: I am innocent without debts, and I am righteous, and there is no sin in me, and I am free from evil
33.10: Behold, he finds occasions against me and he counts me as an enemy to him
33.11: And he laid my feet in the stocks and he watches in all my ways
33.12: In this you cannot be justified; I shall give you an answer: ‘God is greater than man’
33.13: Why have you sued with him, and in all his statements he has not answered?
33.14: Because God speaks with one and does not do more with two, and by the lips he is not understood
33.15: In a dream and in a vision of the night, when sleep falls on man in slumber on a couch
33.16: Then he opens the ear of a man, and in their revolt he will humble them
33.17: To cause a man to pass from his works and to hide the body of a man
33.18: He will save his soul from corruption and his life from destruction
33.19: And he will hide in sickness on his bed and the multitude of his bones will be sore
33.20: His flesh will waste away from his fear, and his soul will not be satisfied with bread, and his soul will lust for food
33.21: He will consume his flesh from fear, and the multitude of his bones he will not see
33.22: And his soul will come near to destruction and his life to death
33.23: If he has an Angel that hears him, one of a thousand, he will show to the man his uprightness
33.24: And he will show compassion upon him and will say: ‘Save him, that he will not descend to destruction and he will find salvation!’
33.25: His flesh shall be transformed as that in his boyhood, and he shall return to the days of his youth
33.26: And he will pray before God and he will answer him, and will be pleased with him and will appear before him in glory, and he will award to a son of man his righteousness
33.27: And Uprightness will speak concerning a man, and he will say: ‘I have sinned, and truly I have offended, and my soul has not prospered
33.28: Save my soul from the destruction of corruption, and my soul will see light!’
33.29: All these things God does three times with a man
33.30: He returns his soul from corruption to see the light of life
33.31: Give ear, Job, and hear, and be silent and I shall speak
33.32: If you have words, bring them to me, because it is desired that you be innocent
33.33: And if not, hear me, be still, and I shall teach you wisdom”

34.2: “Hear my words, wise men, and those who know, pay attention to me
34.3: Because the ear tests words and the palate tastes food
34.4: We will select a Judge for us and he will know what is beautiful between us
34.5: You have said, Job, that you are innocent, for you said: ‘I am justified and God has turned my judgment against me!’
34.6: Who is the man who is destroyed without an offense?
34.7: Who is a man like Job that would drink mockery like water?
34.8: And he was a partner and a friend to workers of evil, walking with men who are sinners
34.9: You said that a man is not just when he is a worshiper of God
34.10: Because of this, men of heart, hear me; God forbid God that he would do evil and God forbid that he would commit sin!
34.11: According to the works of a son of man he pays him, and a man finds according to his ways
34.12: Truly God does no evil, neither does God pervert the way
34.13: Who commanded the Earth and made all the world?
34.14: If he turns his heart against it, his spirit and his soul are gathered to him
34.15: All flesh ends as one, and a son of man returns to his dust
34.16: And if you understand, hear these things and give ear to the voice of my words
34.17: Whoever hates judgment is unjust, and if one is innocent, he is not justly condemned
34.18: He who says evil about a King, a King of Rulers, a King of Governors
34.19: Who will not accept the faces of Princes and will not accept the faces of the poor, because all of them are the work of his hands
34.20: Suddenly they die, and in the middle of the night are consumed and are destroyed and they see The Almighty One, not in power
34.21: Because his eyes are on all the ways of a man and he sees all his steps
34.22: Without darkness and without shadows of death for the Servants of evil to hide themselves there
34.23: Because again, a man was not appointed to walk with God in judgment
34.24: For he has brought evil to many without limit, and we will live together in their place
34.25: Because of this, he shall know them from their works, and he shall turn them away into the night
34.26: And their works will be leveled under evil in the place of power
34.27: For they turned away from after him, and they did not understand all his ways
34.28: The prayer of the poor approaches before him, and the cry of the afflicted he hears
34.29: And if he forgives, who is condemned, and if he turns his face, who is forgiving the people or a son of man together?
34.30: Because a changeable man does not reign, or a sinner over the people
34.31: Because God said: ‘I have forgiven; I do not destroy without sins’
34.32: Teach me if you have done evil
34.33: I shall not again give you an answer, because you have sinned, for you are examined and not I; anything that you know, speak
34.34: Men of heart will speak to me, and wise man will listen to me
34.35: Job was not speaking in knowledge, and his words were not with intelligence
34.36: Truly Job has been tested unto the limit, and he is not considered evil by men
34.37: And if he adds foolishness to his sins among us, he will be commanded and he will bring his words before God

35.2: Were you considered within justice in this, for you said ‘I am more righteous than God?’
35.3: If you spoke, what did it avail you for your soul? And what use that I would condemn you?
35.4: I shall return you words, also to your friends with you
35.5: Gaze into Heaven and search in the clouds that are higher than you
35.6: If you sin, what will you do to him, and if your evil increases, what will you do to him?
