35.7: And if you have been righteous, what do you profit him, or what does he receive from you?
35.8: Your evil is to your soul, and to your soul is your righteousness
35.9: From the abundance of oppression they speak, and they cry out from much evil
35.10: And they do not say: ‘where is God who made us, who gives ideas in the night?
35.11: More than before the animal of the Earth and more than the bird of the air, he teaches us wisdom
35.12: There they cry out and he does not answer them, because of the pride of the evil
35.13: God does not hear uselessly, neither does God praise such
35.14: Even though you have said: “You shall not praise him; sue before him and beg him”
35.15: Now he does not command his anger and he does no evil to the soul
35.16: And Job uselessly opened his mouth and multiplies words without knowledge

36.2: “Wait for me a little and I shall show you, because there are more to God words
36.3: I shall take my knowledge from a distance, and to my Maker I shall give the right
36.4: However, my words truly have been revealed, and my doctrine is without defect with you
36.5: Behold, God is mighty; he does not despise him that is pure as milk
36.6: The evil has no life, and he gives justice to the afflicted
36.7: For the righteous does not fail from before his eyes; he puts Kings on the throne, and they are lifted up for eternities
36.8: And if are they bound in chains, they descend by corruption to poverty
36.9: And he will show them their works and their crimes because they made themselves Masters
36.10: And he will open their ears for instruction and he shall say that they should return from evil
36.11: If they will hear and they will do, also their days will be perfected in goodness, and their years in pleasures
36.12: And if they will not hear, they shall depart into destruction and they will come to an end without knowledge
36.13: And the changeable of heart shall be consumed, and they will not wail in passion; they will wail when he will bind them
36.14: And their soul shall die in youth, and they live in famine
36.15: The afflicted shall escape in his affliction, and he will open their way in oppression
36.16: Also he will deliver you from the mouth of the mocker, and a spacious place is substituted for distress, and he will set a table full of rich food
36.17: And he will judge the wicked entirely with justice, and with justice they will be held captive
36.18: He will not provoke you in the wrath of war, and he will not compel you with the abundance of salvation
36.19: He will be joined to you that he may save you, that you will not be distressed by any mighty in power
36.20: And he who removes one in the night also will give nations for your sake and peoples for the sake of your soul
36.21: Beware that you not be changed by evil; because of this, you are tested in poverty
36.22: God is mighty in power, who teaches like he?
36.23: Who commands concerning his ways, and who says to him: ‘You have done evil?’
36.24: Remember that his works are great, and all men praised him
36.25: And the children of men saw him and they looked from a distance
36.26: Behold, God is mighty and is not known, and the numbering of his years has no end
36.27: If he will number the pillars of Heaven and will bind the drops of rain by himself
36.28: That pour down the sky in time, and clouds sprinkle on the children of men, and they will rejoice greatly
36.29: And who will understand and spreads a cloud from the abundance of his tabernacle?
36.30: He spreads his light over them and he waters the covering of the sea
36.31: Because by them he will judge the nations and he will give food to the many
36.32: And with the hands he will cover the light, and he will go forth upon them that they may meet with him
36.33: He will show his possessions to his friends, also to the evil

37.1: Also, for this, his heart will be moved and will move from its place
37.2: Hear surely his voice in passion, the judgment that proceeds from his mouth
37.3: Under all Heaven they will praise him, and his light upon the edges of the Earth
37.4: In his place he will thunder with a voice of majesty, and he will not search them, because they hear his voice
37.5: And God will thunder with his voice; he does wonders, also great things, and he is unknown
37.6: Because he says to the snow: ‘Be on the Earth, and the rain and the great spring rain’
37.7: By every man he certifies to make his works known to every man
37.8: And the wild beast will enter into a lair and shall dwell in its den
37.9: From the inner chambers will come the whirlwind, and from a violent rain, the cold
37.10: And from the breath of God will be given ice crystal, and a multitude of waters going down
37.11: And gently are spread out the clouds, and he scatters the clouds of his light
37.12: And it turns around and it returns to perform counsel, everything that he commands them on the face of the world of his Earth
37.13: For if for the Leaders or if for the land or if for the holy that are found in it
37.14: Give ear to this, Job, and hear, and discern the wonders of God
37.15: Behold, do you know the thing that God appointed upon them and he has revealed the light of his clouds?
37.16: Do you know the going forth of clouds and the wonders of him who is without defect in knowledge?
37.17: Your garments will be warm and the Earth will move from the south
37.18: Will you spread out the mighty sky with him to uphold together?
37.19: Teach me what I shall say to him, and we shall not be destroyed from before the darkness
37.20: And I shall tell him what I shall say, and if a man will speak, he would be swallowed up
37.21: And now they do not see the light; it is illuminated in the sky of Heaven, and the Spirit will pass and purify them
37.22: From the North gold will come, and from God, light of The Almighty One
37.23: Great power and judgment and great innocent ones he will not answer
37.24: Because of this, the people will fear and all the wise of heart will be shaken by him

38.1: And LORD JEHOVAH answered and said to Job from a hurricane:
38.2: “Who is this who devises doctrine in words without knowledge?
38.3: Wrap your garment now on your loins as a mighty man. I shall ask you; teach me!
38.4: Where were you when I was laying the foundations of the Earth? Show me, if you know understanding
38.5: Who set down its measurements, if you know, and who has stretched a cord upon it?
38.6: On what have its ends stood, or who laid the stone in its corner?
38.7: He created as one the stars of the morning and all the sons of the Angels shouted for joy
38.8: And he shut the doors of the sea and it gushed out from the womb and it brought forth
38.9: When he set the work of the clouds her clothing, and thick darkness its swaddling band
38.10: And he made for her a covenant, and he made doors and lock bolts
38.11: And I said: ‘unto here you shall come and not go beyond, and here you will stay in the magnificence of your waves’
38.12: From your days have you commanded the dawn? Do you know which is the place of early dawn
38.13: To seize on the outer coverings of Earth, and the wicked shall be thrown out of it?
38.14: Their bodies shall be changed like clay, and they shall rise as clothing
38.15: Their light shall be withheld from sinners, and an high arm will be broken
38.16: Have you entered to the depths of the sea, and in the foundations of the Abyss have you walked?
38.17: Were the doors of death revealed to you and the doors of the shadows of death?
38.18: Have you seen all the breadth of the Earth? Show me if you know
38.19: In which path does the light dwell, and what is the place of darkness
38.20: If you know her border and the paths of her house
38.21: And if you know when you were conceived, and the number of your days, if they are many?
38.22: Have you entered the storehouses of the snow and do you see the storehouses of hail
38.23: Which are kept for the time of trouble and the day of conflict of war?
38.24: Or by which path is the light divided and the wind goes out on the Earth?
38.25: And there was an appearance and a sound
38.26: To send down rain on the land without a man, and in the wilderness where is no man
38.27: To satisfy every tangle of thorns and to make the procession of spring, spring up
38.28: Is there a father for the rain, or who begot the drops
38.29: And the dew and ice? From whose belly did they go out? And who begot the frost of Heaven?
38.30: The waters are hardened like stone, and the surfaces of the depths are frozen
38.31: Have you seized on the face of the Pleiades, or have you seen the path of the GiantOrion?
38.32: Behold, shall you bring forth the Waggoner in its time, or have you stood at the face of the star Aldebaran?
38.33: Do you know the law of Heaven, or do you lay down the law in the earth?
38.34: Do you lift up your voice over the clouds, and do you cover them with a multitude of waters?
38.35: Are you sending lightnings and they go and say to you: ‘Here we are!’?
38.36: Who set wisdom in a hiding place, or who gave vision to discernment?
38.37: Who numbered the clouds in his wisdom, and who established the pillars of Heaven?
38.38: Who poured the dust on the Earth, and who fashioned the cliffs?
38.39: Who gives a lion food and has filled the soul of the lions?
38.40: Who multiplies animals in the wilderness
38.41: And who gives the raven her food, because her children cry out to God and are failing from lack of food?

39.1: Do you know the time that the wild goats on a cliff bear young, and the bearing of the hinds?
39.2: Do you keep the numbers of the months and do you know the time of their delivery
39.3: And when they kneel and give birth
39.4: And they rear their children and they are weaned?
39.5: Who sent away the wild donkey a son of the free, and lets him escape from the yoke
39.6: That makes the valley his house, and his dwelling in the place of salt?
39.7: He laughs at the multitude of the cities and is not afraid of the voice of Rulers
39.8: In the multitude of the mountains is his pasture, and he treads upon every green thing
39.9: Does a wild bull consent to serve you, or does he spend the night at your stall?
39.10: Do you bind a yoke on the neck of the wild ox and he drives a plow in the rugged place?
39.11: Do you trust upon him because his power is great, and do you leave your labors upon him?
39.12: Do you trust him to thresh your granary and gather your seed?
39.13: The glorious wings of the peacock are made great, and she comes and makes a nest
39.14: And she leaves her daughters on the ground, and warms them upon the dust
39.15: And she has forgotten that she has the foot of a bird of prey, and the animal of the Earth treads on it
39.16: She has multiplied children that are not with her, and she has labored uselessly without fear
39.17: Because God has increased wisdom and he has not distributed understanding to her
39.18: She will be lifted up high as a palm tree; she will laugh at the horse and at his rider
39.19: Did you gave the horse manly strength, and did you cloth his neck with armor?
39.20: Do you shake him like a grasshopper, or do you terrify him with fear?
39.21: He paws in the depths and prances in the valley and goes out in armor for the attack
39.22: He laughs at a pit and is not afraid, and he does not turn from the face of the sword
39.23: The quiver is glorious against him and the flash of the spear and of the javelin
39.24: He runs on the Earth with shaking and with passion and is not afraid of the sound of the trumpet
39.25: With a voice he says, ‘Hurrah!’ and from a distance he smells the war and shakes Princes with his whinnying
39.26: Does the hawk exist from your wisdom and spread his wings to the south?
39.27: Will the eagle be lifted up by the word of your mouth and set up his nest on the cliff?
39.28: And he dwells and lodges on a crag of a cliff
39.29: And he is sustained from his prey, and his eyes gaze a long distance
39.30: And his chicks lick up blood, and where the slain are killed, there he is”

40.1: And LORD JEHOVAH answered and said to Job:
40.2: “Many are the counselors of God, and he who rebukes God gives an answer!”
40.3: And Job answered and said to LORD JEHOVAH:
40.4: “I have come short to myself. What shall I return to you? I have put my hands on my mouth
40.5: Once I have spoken and I shall not give an answer, and I will not continue to speak a second time!”
40.6: And LORD JEHOVAH answered and said to Job from the hurricane:
40.7: “Wrap now your loins as a mighty man. I shall ask you; teach me
40.8: If it is that you nullify me and you condemn me that you will be justified
40.9: Do you have an arm like God, and do you thunder with a voice like his?
40.10: Wear magnificence and heroism and put on brightness and excellence
40.11: And pour out the heat of your wrath and see everything that is exalted and pull it down
40.12: And cast down sinners in their places
40.13: And bury them together in the dust and cover their faces with dust
40.14: Also I shall praise you when your right hand has saved you
40.15: Behold, Behemoth which I have made with you; he eats grass like an ox
40.16: And his strength is in his hindquarters
40.17: He lifts up his tail like a cedar and the sinews of his thighs stand up
40.18: His bones are strong as brass and like iron
40.19: He is the chief of all the creation of God, for he made him that he would make war
40.20: And the abundance of the mountains carry to him, and all the animals of the wilderness
40.21: Under its shade he lies down; in the shelter of the rushes he crouches
40.22: The shadows surround him, and the ravens of the valley surround him
40.23: If the river rises, he is not shaken and trusts that he may pour the Jordan into his mouth
40.24: In his clouds let them take him, and in the net let him be held captive

41.1: Will you hold the Dragon in a net by a cord? Will you hold him by his tongue?
41.2: Do you cast a bridle in his mouth, and will you pierce his cheek with a thorn?
41.3: And if he begs much from you and speaks to you tenderly
41.4: Does he establish a covenant with you, or do you account him as a Servant forever?
41.5: Do you play with him like a bird and keep him for the days of your youth?
41.6: Shall companions be gathered for him and divide him among many?
41.7: Do you fill his skin with meat and his head in the shadow of fire
41.8: Lay your hand upon him and you shall have no more memory of war
41.9: Now your foot is released, also God shall take away your bitterness
41.10: He will not go far when he is awakened, and who is going to stand before me?
41.11: Who is before me that I would surrender? All under Heaven is mine!
41.12: And I shall not be silent at his strength and at the sinews of his power
41.13: Who opens the face of his clothing, and when he attacks the net, who comes?
41.14: Who opens the door of his mouth? The row of his teeth is awesome!
41.15: For in the torrent his mouth is tied up and bound and sealed
41.16: They are touching one another, and air does not enter between them
41.17: They are joined one to another and shut and are not separated
41.18: And his eyes are filled with light, and his eyes are like the bright shining dawn
41.19: And lamps go out of his mouth as coals of fire, and are burning like rays of fire
41.20: From his nostrils smoke goes out as the flame of a caldron which is seething
41.21: His breath kindles burning coals and flames come out from his mouth
41.22: On his neck lodges strength, and before him fear will dance for joy
41.23: His flesh is glorious and strong and firmly knit and unmoved
41.24: His heart is firm as stone and it is hard as quartz
41.25: From his awesomeness the mighty will be moved and the strong will be laid low
41.26: The cut of the sword cannot remain; he bears the lances of great ones
41.27: Iron is considered as straw to him, and brass is as rotten wood with a boring worm in it
41.28: He does not flee from the bow, and the stones of a sling are considered by him like chips
41.31: And the deep is considered like dry land to him
41.33: And his power that causes destruction walks on the dust
41.34: He sees all that is high, and he is King over every creeping thing

42.1: And Job answered and said to LORD JEHOVAH:
42.2: “I Know that you can do all these things, and no mind is hidden from you
42.3: Who is this who devises doctrine by words without knowledge? Because of this you showed me that I did not understand and I have not known things that are greater than I
42.4: Hear me and I shall speak. I shall ask; you teach me
42.5: It is by the hearing of the ear I have heard you, and now, behold, my eye has seen you!
42.6: Because of this I shall be silent and I shall be revived on the dust and on the ashes
42.7: And it was after LORD JEHOVAH spoke these answers with Job, LORD JEHOVAH said to Eliphaz the Taymanite: “My anger was enraged against you and against your two companions, because you have not spoken righteousness before me as my Servant Job
42.8: Now take to yourself seven oxen and seven rams and go to my Servant Job, and he shall offer up a gift in exchange for yourselves, and my Servant Job shall pray for you, and I shall serve his person so that I will not cause you shame, because you have not spoken righteousness before me as my Servant Job”
42.9: And Eliphaz the Taymanite, Beldad the Shukhite and Tsuphar the Namathite went and they did as LORD JEHOVAH said to them, and LORD JEHOVAH served the person of Job
42.10: And LORD JEHOVAH turned back the captivity of Job when he was praying for his friends, and LORD JEHOVAH added double to everything that Job had possessed before
42.11: And all his brothers and his sisters came to him and all who knew him from former time, and they ate bread with him in his house where they had been mourning for him, and they comforted him from all the evil that LORD JEHOVAH brought upon him, and they gave him, each man, a ewe, and each one a nose ring of gold
42.12: And LORD JEHOVAH blessed the end of Job more than his beginning, and he had fourteen thousand sheep and six thousand camels and a thousand yokes of oxen and a thousand she donkeys
42.13: And he had seven sons and three daughters
42.14: And he called the name of one of them Yemema, and the name of the second, Qetsua, and the name of the third, Qarnphuuk
42.15: And there were not found women who were as beautiful the daughters of Job in all the land, and their father gave them an inheritance with their brothers
42.16: And Job lived a hundred and forty years after that, and he saw his sons, and his grandsons, unto four generations
42.17: Job was old and his days were full, and he died
